title: Chapter 2352: Indestructible Flesh Forging Scripture
content:
Chapter 2352: Indestructible Flesh Forging Scripture

Yuan and Kulas watched with wide eyes as Ren Xia approached them with calm, graceful steps. She was tall and slender, her light pink hair reminiscent of cherry blossoms, her figure refined and well-proportioned, like an hourglass.

A faint frown creased Ren Xia’s beautiful face, one that rivaled even Yu Ning, renowned as one of the greatest beauties in the Nine Heavens, and she spoke, "What’s with that reaction? You look like you’re staring at a ghost."

"If you’re alive, why would it be strange for me to be alive as well?"

Before they could respond, Ren Xia turned to look at Yuan and continued, "For you to be surprised as well, I guess you haven’t fully recovered your memories yet, my dear."

"Unfortunately, I only remember up to when I defeated Kulas during my life as Tian Yang," he replied with an apologetic smile.

"Sorry."

Ren Xia silently stared at him for a good moment before releasing a deep sigh.

"It’s not like this is the first time I’ve been through this," she then said.

"Why don’t you just tell him?" Kulas asked.

"You’re just as clueless, huh?" Ren Xia shook her head at Kulas.

She proceeded to explain, "To keep it brief and simple, those who are powerful enough to control their own fate could alter others’ fate in unexpected ways. You know the saying, let fate take its course? I could alter that course for him if I interfere too much."

"I still don’t get it." Kulas shook his head.

Ren Xia rubbed her eyes and sighed.

"He will naturally recover his memories even if I don’t do anything because that’s his fate. However, if I intervene and help him remember much earlier than he was fated to, it would alter his fate."

Hearing this explanation, Kulas finally understood the situation. However, he still had questions.

"I understand that it might alter his future, but is that really such a bad thing? Only the truly powerful take control of their own fate, and knowing my brother, he’s definitely strong enough to do that, so couldn’t he just fix his own fate if it goes awry?"

"Of course, he can. However, look at him right now. Only those who become a Cultivation God can possibly control their own fate, and he’s not even in the God Ascension Realm. Even if he has the potential to reach that level, it might already be too late by then."

"For example, his fate is altered so much that he perishes before reaching Cultivation God, or something happens and prevents him from reaching that level. There are too many variables, so it’s best to avoid them altogether by being careful."

"Anyway, I have said enough. If you still don’t understand it, you’re a lost cause."

Kulas shook his head, a bittersweet smile tugging at his lips.

"You surviving so many eras is impressive," he remarked. "But what really amazes me is how your nasty attitude managed to last just as long."

"Oh? You think my attitude is nasty now? Let me show you just how nasty I can be!"

Without warning, Ren Xia lunged at Kulas. Before he could even react, her hands had already clamped around his neck. Instinctively, he struck at her arm in an attempt to break free—but the moment his blow landed, it felt as though he had hit solid metal.

"When did you become a body refiner?!" he exclaimed in shock.

"Do you have any idea how long I have been alive? With how much time I have, it’s only natural for me to try out different things!" Ren Xia explained as she casually tossed Kulas into the air.

"At my current level, I dare say that I have long surpassed your peak, Kulas!"

Kulas tried to stabilize himself in the air, but he found it impossible, as Ren Xia had enveloped his body with her Celestial Qi.

"Damn it! Why do you hate me so much?!" Kulas cried out loud. "I don’t remember offending you to such an extent!"

"Hate you? I don’t hate you," Ren Xia replied in a calm voice.

"Bullshit! If you didn’t hate me, why are you always trying to argue with me?! Are you seriously that jealous of my brotherly bond with Tian Yang?! Or perhaps, you actually like me—"

BOOM!

As if a mountain had been dropped onto him, Kulas suddenly fell from the sky and slammed into the ground without mercy.

"I mess with you because my dear is too kind to lecture you. Despite all your foolishness in the past, he’d never once complained about your pain in the ass, and you’re taking his kindness for granted—at least that’s how it looks to me."

Kulas tried pushing himself off the ground, but Ren Xia’s pressure was too overbearing for someone without cultivation like him.

"T-This pressure... You’re a Cultivation God!" Kulas muttered with gritted teeth.

Despite his loud voice, he wasn’t actually surprised by this.

He continued, "I know how much trouble I’ve caused you and my brother. That’s why I am going to spend the rest of this life that he granted me to repay you two... if you give me the chance!"

Ren Xia suddenly released her pressure on him.

"Your words mean nothing in your current state," she sneered. "Forget repaying us. You can’t even protect yourself the way you are now."

"T-That’s why I will train hard to—"

Ren Xia suddenly flicked her sleeves, throwing a manual directly into Kulas’ face.

Kulas subconsciously caught the manual and read the title on the cover.

"Indestructible Flesh Forging Scripture?"

"It’s one of the strongest body tempering techniques in the world right now. It was created by the Indestructible Titan, who is known as the most powerful body refiner to exist. Of course, not even the Great Mammoth Body Refinement technique could compare to it."

Kulas stared at her, his eyes and mouth agape with shock and disbelief.

A moment of silence later, he asked, "Did you acquire this technique specifically for—"

"Don’t get full of yourself."

Without hesitation, Ren Xia immediately denied it before he could even finish his sentence, leaving him speechless. Despite all that, Kulas was feeling nothing but delight at this moment.

"Thank you..." he said.
